Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun icon|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Sites}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." {{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} | alternate_name = | group = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}{{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyake | latitude = 32.1218778 | longitude = 131.3844778 | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | dimensions = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | construction_period = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map2 = {{Location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun icon|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Sites}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

{{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} | alternate_name = | group = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}{{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyake | latitude = 32.1218778 | longitude = 131.3844778 | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | dimensions = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | construction_period = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map2 = {{Location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun icon|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Sites}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | construction_period = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map2 = {{Location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun icon|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Sites}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:newer pages]][[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | construction_period = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map2 = {{Location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun icon|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Sites}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:newer pages]][[Category:newer pages]][[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:newer pages]][[Category:newer pages]][[Category:newer pages]][[Category:newer pages]][[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | construction_period = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu

Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun icon|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Sites}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:dardar]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | construction_period = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map2 = {{Location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun icon|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{Location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Sites}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * Location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]

Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun icon|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Sites}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}}, designated as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Site," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image2 = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 burial mounds, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it forms the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated burial mounds ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which form an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=15-28}} == Artifacts == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|pp=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == Notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]]
