Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | built = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Aerial view (1974)
{{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}{{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyake | coordinates = 32.1218778 | coordinates = 131.3844778 | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | built = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ozasahozuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ozasahozuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:%E7%94%B7%E7%8B%AD%E7%A9%82%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Osahozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|280px]]
Aerial view (1974)
{{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}{{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyake | coordinates = 32.1218778 | coordinates = 131.3844778 | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | built = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Otoko Sasaho-zuka But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ozasahozuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ozasahozuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:%E7%94%B7%E7%8B%AD%E7%A9%82%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Osahozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|280px]]
Aerial view (1974)
{{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}{{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyake | coordinates = 32.1218778 | coordinates = 131.3844778 | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | built = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru {{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}{{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyake | coordinates = 32.1218778 | coordinates = 131.3844778 | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | built = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ozasahozuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ozasahozuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:%E7%94%B7%E7%8B%AD%E7%A9%82%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Osahozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|280px]]
Aerial view (1974)
{{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}{{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyake | coordinates = 32.1218778 | coordinates = 131.3844778 | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | built = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=24|coordinates=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=21|coordinates=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=57|coordinates=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=24|coordinates=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|ja|横瀬古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=21|coordinates=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=57|coordinates=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|ja|生目古墳群}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito City|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun}}, other_designation1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun
