[[co:commons:Category:Osahozuka Kofun]][[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Osahozuka Kofun]][[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Osahozuka Kofun]][[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Osahozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|280px]]
Aerial view (1974)
{{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}{{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito City}}, Miyake | coordinates = 32.1218778 | coordinates = 131.3844778 | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | built = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Osahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=24|coordinates=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|en|Yokose Kofun|WD=Q11543317|lt=Yokose Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=21|coordinates=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Touniotsuka Kofun|1=Tojin Otsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864461}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=57|coordinates=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|en|Ikime Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11574825|lt=Ikime Kofun Cluster}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito City}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitobaru Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|1=Miyako City|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453245}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|version=100256357|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Osahozuka Kofun}} [[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] [[en:Ozasahozuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11578168}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|1=Miyako City|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453245}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|version=100256357|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Osahozuka Kofun}} [[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] [[en:Ozasahozuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11578168}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Aerial view (1974)
{{small|{{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}.}} | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} (Maruyama Subcluster) | location = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}{{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito City|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyake | coordinates = 32.1218778 | coordinates = 131.3844778 | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Length: 176 m (estimated reconstruction)
Height: 19.1 m (circular mound) | built = Early 5th century | burial_facilities = Unknown | honoree = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} estimation) {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | mausoleum = Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Osahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" | notable = Largest kofun in Kyushu
Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=24|coordinates=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|en|Yokose Kofun|WD=Q11543317|lt=Yokose Kofun|1=Yokose Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=21|coordinates=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Touniotsuka Kofun|1=Tojin Otsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864461}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=57|coordinates=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|en|Ikime Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11574825|lt=Ikime Kofun Cluster|1=Ikime Kofun Cluster}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito City|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitobaru Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|1=Miyako City|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453245}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|version=100256357|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Osahozuka Kofun}} [[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] [[en:Ozasahozuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11578168}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|1=Miyako City|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453245}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|version=100256357|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Osahozuka Kofun}} [[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] [[en:Ozasahozuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11578168}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Largest scallop-shaped kofun nationwide | map = {{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=18.76|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.12|map_dot_label='''Osahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark={{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja|帆立貝形古墳-南南東.svg|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun type|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|marksize=36}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=24|coordinates=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label={{ill|Yokose Kofun|en|Yokose Kofun|WD=Q11543317|lt=Yokose Kofun|1=Yokose Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=21|coordinates=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label={{ill|Touniotsuka Kofun|ja|唐仁大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Touniotsuka Kofun|1=Tojin Otsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864461}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|coordinates=31|coordinates=57|coordinates=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikime Kofun Cluster|en|Ikime Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11574825|lt=Ikime Kofun Cluster|1=Ikime Kofun Cluster}}|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} }} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}s}}]] '''Osahozuka Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}, Osahozuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito City|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, specifically within the Maruyama Subcluster, and is the largest {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Japan. While the actual honoree is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates this site as the "Osahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" and identifies {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} as the presumed honoree. It is adjacent to the {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as the "Mesahozuka {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," which is believed to honor Ninigi’s consort, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. == Overview == {{Multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 180 | image1 = [[File:Osahodzuka air.jpg|frameless]] | caption1 = Aerial view | image = [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|frameless]] | caption2 = Entrance to the site }} Osahozuka Kofun is located in the central part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Plateau|ja|西都原台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitobaru Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, on the right bank of the Hitotsuse River. The site is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, which includes over 320 Kofuns, making it the largest kofun cluster in Kyushu. Together with the adjacent Mesahozuka Kofun, it types the Maruyama Subcluster along with three smaller associated Kofuns ({{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}).{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a relatively short front section oriented towards the south-southeast. The circular mound is built in three tiers, while the front section has two tiers. The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length: Approximately176 m * Circular mound diameter: 132 m * Circular mound height: 19.1 m The surrounding moat system includes two concentric moats, which type an incomplete loop around the mound, missing the front section. Excavations have uncovered fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. However, the burial chamber has not been investigated.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} The construction of Osahozuka Kofun is estimated to have taken place in the early 5th century during the middle kofun period, coinciding with the construction of Mesahozuka Kofun. These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|1=Miyako City|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453245}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|version=100256357|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Osahozuka Kofun}} [[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] [[en:Ozasahozuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11578168}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

These twin kofun represent the peak of power consolidation in the region.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=15-28}} == burial goods == Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been recovered from Osahozuka Kofun. These include cylindrical haniwa, notable for their thin walls, fine details, and a reddish-brown coloration indicative of kiln firing. The style is distinct from the open-fire-fired haniwa found at Mesahozuka Kofun.{{Harvnb|Higashi|2017|11=29-62}} == Honoree == While the precise identity of the buried individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} attributes the burial to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, a deity from Japanese mythology. This attribution is based on records from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, which mention Ninigi’s tomb as being in "Kawai-yama of Hyuga" but do not specify its exact location.{{Cite book|title=Encyclopedia of Imperial Mausolea|author=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2005}} == Access == * location: Miyazaki Prefecture, Saito City, Miyake * Access: From {{ill|Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakoh City Bus Terminal|1=Miyako City|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453245}}, take a Miyazaki Kōtsū bus to "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." Alternatively, use a taxi from Saito Bus Center. == notes == {{reflist|group="note"}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Higashi Kenshō, "The Magnates of Southern Kyushu: Saitobaru Kofun Cluster," Shinzen-sha, 2017. * Miyazaki Prefecture Board of Education, "Reports on Geophysical Surveys of Saitobaru Kofun," 2007. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Mesahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|1=Mezasahozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesahozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|version=100256357|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Osahozuka Kofun}} [[ja:男狭穂塚古墳]] [[en:Ozasahozuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11578168}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} [[ja:男狭稲塚古坛]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
